critical.title: PLE critically low: 278s
critical.description: Buffer pool is churning. Add memory or reduce IO.

critical.title: Log backup gap > 24h: shopdb
critical.description: Recovery model is FULL, last log backup 34.7 hours ago. Log will grow unbounded.
critical.recommendation: BACKUP LOG [shopdb] TO DISK='...' WITH COMPRESSION;

critical.title: VLF count 14,820 on shopdb
critical.description: Startup, log backups, and crash recovery will be slow.
critical.recommendation: USE shopdb; DBCC SHRINKFILE (shopdb_log, 100); ALTER DATABASE shopdb MODIFY FILE (NAME = shopdb_log, SIZE = 8192MB, FILEGROWTH = 8192MB);

critical.title: Missing index on dbo.customers (email)
critical.recommendation: CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_customers_email ON dbo.customers (email) INCLUDE (first_name, last_name, created_at);

critical.title: 7,200 ms avg · 18,400 runs
critical.description: SELECT o.id, o.customer_id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total FROM dbo.orders o JOIN dbo.order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id WHE...
critical.recommendation: SELECT o.id, o.customer_id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total FROM dbo.orders o JOIN dbo.order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.created_at > DATEADD(day, -30, GETDATE()) GROUP BY o.id, o.customer_id

details.blocker_query: BEGIN TRAN; SELECT TOP (10000) * FROM dbo.events WITH (TABLOCKX); ...
details.blocked_query: INSERT INTO dbo.events (user_id, type, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)

details.blocker_query: UPDATE dbo.orders SET status = 'shipped' WHERE id = 1234567

info.title: ASYNC_NETWORK_IO — 7.2%
info.description: Client slow to consume results. Almost always client-side.

info.title: CXCONSUMER — 2.1%
info.description: Parallelism consumer wait. Not actionable on its own.

info.title: CXPACKET — 22.5%
info.description: Parallelism coordination. Usually benign. Check MAXDOP only if dominant.

info.title: LCK_M_S — 3.2%
info.description: Shared lock wait. Readers blocked by writers. Consider RCSI.

info.title: LCK_M_X — 8.5%
info.description: Exclusive lock wait. Look at current blocking and long transactions.

info.title: PAGELATCH_EX — 4.8%
info.description: In-memory page contention, typically tempdb allocation. Bump tempdb file count.

info.title: RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE — 1.2%
info.description: Memory grant queue. Critical when sustained.

info.title: SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD — 6.1%
info.description: CPU pressure. Top CPU consumers; parallelism.

info.title: WRITELOG — 12.3%
info.description: Log writer flush wait. Log IO latency. Faster log storage or smaller txns.
